mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the CPIAUCSL (inflation) signals reduced monetary policy urgency, lowering discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL declines (Rank IC < 0.02) or exhibit negative returns in 60%+ of low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in UNRATE below 5% indicates tightening labor markets, increasing corporate earnings potential and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fail to outperform baseline in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in INDPRO (industrial production) and HOUST (housing starts) signals robust supply-side expansion, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between INDPRO and HOUST fails to explain 15%+ of SPX 20d return variance (R-squared < 0.15) in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 (GDP) following a recessionary trough indicates recovering demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 rebound periods show no significant outperformance (t-test p > 0.10) compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) growth signals strong employment conditions, driving consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.01) in 80%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month surge in RSAFS (real personal income) above 2% YoY indicates elevated consumer spending capacity, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in RSAFS surge periods show no significant outperformance (t-test p > 0.05) compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal aggressive monetary easing amid deflationary risks, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL explains < 10% of SPX 20d return variance (R-squared < 0.10) in easing environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% indicates monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS >4.5% periods show no significant negative correlation (Rank IC > -0.02) or exhibit positive returns in 60%+ of cases.
